content.full_text:
TO: [✓] JEANNE   [ ] JAY
[✓] LYNN   [ ] BARBARA
[ ] RICH   [ ] BOB S.
[✓] HELEN   [✓] OTHER Carla

FROM: Mary Ann
DATE: August 17 '83

[ ] TELEPHONE ADVICE - SEE BELOW
[ ] CORRESPONDENCE ADVICE - SEE ATTACHED DRAFT
(IF YOU HAVE ANY COMMENTS, NOTIFY ADVISOR WITHIN 1 DAY)

FILE LOCATION:

INCLUDE IN ADVICE PACKAGE: YES [ ] NO ☒

CALLER Mr. Ledford
REPRESENTING Frazee's Office
PHONE NUMBER: 619/729-8504

QUESTION: Does a loan which has previously been reported trigger a threshold statement when the loan is forgiven during a subsequent threshold period? All other criteria for having to file a threshold statement have been met.

ANSWER: No. The loan has already been reported. The contributor will need to report the forgiveness on Schedule E on the next required threshold filing or semi-annual statement itemizing the recipient of the loan with an explanation in the description column that the loan was forgiven (but not entering the amount in the "Amount Paid" column).
